Topic 4: Groyper salute

[132:23]but

[132:24]She's writing the profile, and she says that she sits down at dinner with him, and he does a Hitler salute.

[132:47]In the lead, in the first paragraph, she goes, he's getting his doctorate.

[135:59]they get their guys together,

Topic 5: Another hit-piece

[137:44]You do recognize that all of these pieces it's another hit piece by a different way

[137:52]When they write these pieces and they say that I'm winning and Gropers are winning

[137:58]You might read that in a triumphant way,

[138:01]and certainly it's good propaganda.

[138:03]So there's this piece in the Atlantic last week

[138:05]it said, Nick Fuentes' strategy is working.

[138:08]And you might read that and say,

[138:10]see, the media is admitting that he's winning

[138:12]because that's how undeniable it is.

[138:15]You must understand,

[138:17]this is a subtle way of writing a hit piece.

[138:22]And this is how the media operates

[138:24]because the media, they're smart.

[138:27]And by saying that the bad guys are winning,

[138:30]what they're really doing is you're trying to galvanize

[138:34]the good guys to destroy the bad guys.

[138:36]So if they go on the Atlantic and say,

[138:39]Nick Fuentes is unstoppable on Instagram,

[138:41]he's winning, he's breaking through the algorithm.

[138:46]You might read that and say,

[138:48]oh, they're acknowledging your triumph.

[138:51]What they're really doing is they're begging Instagram

[138:54]to ban me.

[138:55]Because they're saying look are you really gonna let this happen Instagram is letting this Nazi win see

[139:03]This Nazi is winning and Instagram is letting him do it and

[139:08]So it's actually meant to galvanize and activate more opposition where there wasn't any

[139:14]Where we were kind of getting away with it

[139:18]Not really. I mean we're posting clips like anybody is

[139:22]But where we were having success and we found a formula that worked, now they're sending

[139:29]up the signal flare telling Instagram, whoa, whoa, whoa, you got a Nazi winning on your

[139:33]platform.

[139:34]You better put your finger on the scale.

[139:35]You better change the rules.

[139:37]So that's an example of it last week.

[139:40]And it's the same thing here.

[139:45]what they're really trying to do is galvanize

[139:47]the right-wing establishment to destroy

[139:50]the groypers in government.

[140:15]and they could train and refine the right wing

[140:18]with that kind of a guilt by association.

[140:27]I've been doing this for a long time

[140:29]and that's clearly the end game there.

[140:34]And I happen to believe at this point

[140:36]it's just a little bit too late.

Topic 6: Part 1 (Trump's twisted meltdown, his wickedness & his self-indulgence)

[141:22]the elephant in the room with our coverage of Iran,

[141:25]the latest is this post yesterday

[141:28]on true social from the president.

[141:32]about the significance of it in a minute

[141:34]from the point of view of the conflict

[141:36]and how it's unfolding and where all this is gonna go.

[141:45]pointed out, there's something really striking about it.

[141:55]He said quote Tuesday will be power plant day and bridge day all wrapped up in one in Iran

[142:37]about this sort of thing, but it's everything about this.

[145:10]Trump.

[145:12]What kind of human being writes something like this?

[145:20]and beyond.

[145:21]I used to think 10 years ago that when Trump would fight back against the media, this is

[145:27]one of the things that we used to love about Trump.

[145:34]that he was rough around the edges.

[145:36]He's direct.

[145:37]He talks how we talk.

[145:40]He says what we're thinking.

[145:42]He's not politically correct.

[145:45]That used to be a part of the appeal.

[145:47]Actually, that used to be central to his appeal.

[145:49]People used to say that that was an indicator

[145:52]of his authenticity, his honesty, his groundedness.

[145:56]He's not like the other politicians.

[145:59]He's not about the bullshit.

[146:02]And so, 10 years ago, I used to think that was funny.

[146:06]I used to think it was cool.

[146:08]I used to think it was refreshing.

[146:10]And the reason is because it seemed that all of these civility and the formalities and

[146:16]everything like that, it seemed like all of that, we were going through the motions.

[146:22]And underlying it was a lot of propaganda and lies.

[146:32]Let's dispense with all of the pomp and the formalities.

[146:36]Let's dispense with all of this politically correct language.

[146:39]And let's just be direct and honest about what's happened again in our country.

[146:42]And maybe there's something to be said about that.

[146:45]But we have come all the way around over the course of 10 years and now is there no decorum,

[146:53]is nothing sacred, is there ever a time to be respectful of anyone or anything?

[147:00]How about respectful of the US population?

[147:04]How about the fact that you're the US president for children and families and for a nation

[147:13]How about respectful of the fact that it's a holiday, that it's Easter, respectful of God?

[147:21]There's a time and a place for everything that you could argue.

[147:26]There's a time when we can say a swear word and there's a time when we can be funny or flippant.

[147:36]when you're at war and it concerns the lives and deaths

[147:40]of U.S. soldiers and civilians.

[147:46]on Easter, the one day of the year,

[147:49]when we commemorate the resurrection of Jesus Christ.

[147:55]but Easter is the actual historical date when it happens.

Topic 7: Part 2 (our loyalty, our movement & it's our turn)

[156:06]Because the one thing that is unique about the Gropers compared to everybody else

[156:11]everybody else is

[156:13]That my followers have always been more loyal to me than they are to Trump

[156:19]And that is the difference, because I'll tell you something, everybody else, everybody

[156:26]else, including and especially those that are against this war in Iran and always have

[156:34]been, including, and especially those that are not in support of this immigration policy

[156:40]or unhappy with it, they all supported Trump in 2024.

[156:48]And I'm talking about Candace, and I'm talking about Ian Carroll and Dave Smith and Alex

[156:55]Jones.

[156:56]And I like a lot of these people, and I think a lot of these people are doing a really phenomenal

[157:00]job right now.

[157:02]But all these people, to a man or woman, supported Trump in 24, either because they were caught

[157:10]up in it or because they had no other choice.

[157:13]They couldn't oppose the president why because Trump is more popular than them

[157:19]Trump is more popular than them with their own followers. So they had to

[157:25]It would have been career suicide for anyone else to go against Trump

[157:31]before it was popular.

[157:33]Now some of these people, you could see now Tucker is breaking, Candice is breaking, Dave

[157:38]is calling for Trump to be impeached, and credit where it is due.

[157:43]Better late than never.

[157:45]But they had to wait for it to get this bad on some level because it maybe wasn't an option

[157:52]in 2024 because they were subsidiary to the larger Trump umbrella.

[157:58]And for 10 years, that is why it has been so important for us to have our own thing.

[158:04]our own bubble, insulated, distinct, separate, protected,

[158:10]and sometimes that made people feel uncomfortable.

[158:13]Sometimes that made people feel left out.

[158:16]They wanted to be a part of the Trump movement.

[158:18]They wanted the benefits conferred upon them

[158:21]by the Trump movement.

[158:22]They didn't wanna be out here in this other club,

[158:26]but that is why we always had to go that way,

[158:29]because the writing was on the wall even eight years ago.

[158:33]I remember, and this is the last thing I'll say, and then we're going to move on.

[158:37]I remember in the Groyper War in 2019, when Groypers were invented, when the Groypers

[158:44]became synonymous with my followers, when America first got put on the map, we raised

[158:49]the challenge to Charlie Kirk, when all of this started.

[158:52]I remember in 2019, my followers would go to Turning Point and say, you're not a real

[158:58]conservative because you support mass migration, you put Israel over America, and you're a

[159:04]social liberal.

[159:05]So how are you conservative?

[159:07]And you know what they would say?

[159:08]They would say, well, guess what?

[159:11]Trump is in favor of legal immigration and Trump is pro Israel and Trump endorsed Lindsey

[159:17]Graham and Trump is pro gay and pro trans.

[159:20]He's friends with Caitlyn Jenner.

[159:22]And so they would say it is you that is out of step with MAGA.

[159:27]It is you that is a fake Trump supporter.

[159:30]We are the real MAGA.

[159:39]But I said, you know what?

[159:41]You're right.

[159:42]It's true.

[159:44]Lindsey Graham is welcome in Mar-a-Lago.

[159:46]We are not.

[159:48]Doesn't that tell you something?

[159:49]Mark Levin is promoted by Trump.

[159:52]We are not and never will be.

[159:54]Alex Jones got an interview with Trump in 2015.

[159:57]That was the end of that.

[159:58]But the rest of them, Sean Hannity, Levin, they still get access.

[160:04]I said, so instead of forcing it and trying to make it work, I said, we need to recognize

[160:09]that on a long enough timeline, if we put all our eggs in this basket, we're going

[160:14]to find ourselves outside in the cold because you know what?

[160:17]They're right.

[160:18]Mar-a-Lago and the Trump movement always has been infiltrated by those elements.

[160:23]And we're never going to be able to leapfrog them in the court of Trump thinking if we

[160:30]If we defer enough to the president, if we engage in these court politics and political

[160:37]machinations, maybe one day he'll choose John McIntyre over Brooke Rollins.

[172:08]is trying to keep morale up.

[172:18]is frequently making things up

[172:21]or downplaying the duration or severity of the conflict
